A.definition: Comparing two or more versions of an ad campaign element to see which performs better.
A digital marketplace where advertisers and publishers buy and sell ad inventory in real-time without protection of an intermediary.
An invalid activity that prevents ads from reaching real users (e.g. bot traffic, fake clicks or fraudulent impressions).
Total space available for advertisements on a publisher’s platform.
A platform such as Galaksion that connects advertisers looking for ad placements and publishers searching for website monetization.
Delivering ads to specific audiences based on demographics, location, or behavior.
An individual, a company, or a brand that promotes products and services using digital ad campaigns.
Malicious software designed to display unwanted ads on affected devices.
A URL that serves as a unique identifier which tracks the source of web traffic back to a certain affiliate partner.
A marketing strategy where third-party affiliates earn commissions by promoting products or services from online merchants.
An online community where affiliate marketers share ideas.
A partnership where affiliates earn a commission for driving sales.
An international ranking system measuring the website's popularity based on the number of visitors and engagements.
Descriptive text for images and graphics used for accessibility and SEO.
Clickable text in a hyperlink taking the user to a webpage.
A set of programming rules enabling communication between software.
Offers promoting Android Package Kits, a file format used to distribute and install applications for the Android OS.
Offers that automatically download content upon user interaction.

B.definition: An inbound link to a webpage from another site that influences reputabilty and SEO ranking.
A rectangular graphic ad often placed across the top or side of the webpage.
Showing relevant ads to users based on their online activity and browsing patterns (e.g. interactions, purchasing habits, and browsing history).
Unethical SEO tactics to manipulate search engine rankings by violating policies.
A list of underperforming ad zones an advertiser would like to exclude as traffic sources.
Percentage of visitors leaving the website without taking interactions upon entering.
Protection from being assosiated with inappropriate or harmful content.

C.definition: A reversal of a sales transaction upon a disputed charge.
Fraudulent clicks on ads maliciously generated to inflate costs or revenues.
A unique identifier for a specific click event designed to track the user's journey to conversion.
A user clicking on a link or an ad.
Ratio of clicks to the total number of impressions for a link or an ad.
A SEO technique masking content to deceive search engines.
A payment earned by an affiliate partner per customer action, like a sale or lead.
A platform producing large volumes of low-quality content for ad revenue.
Links embedded within the body of a text that contains related information.
A user completing a targeted action (e.g. a purchase, an installation, a sign-up, or filling out a form).
Percentage of users who complete a desired action out of the total number of website visitors.
Small data files stored on a user's device by a website identifying the visit and browsing activity.
Ad payment model based on the number of clicks received.
Ad payment model based on every 1000 ad impressions.
Ad payment model based on the number of desired actions taken.
Ad payment model based on the number of leads generated.
Ad payment model based on the number of sales generated.
Ad payment model based on the number of views the video received.
A bot that scans websites to index content and ensure the proper functionality of affiliate links.
Images, videos, text, and other design elements used to convey the message of an ad and stimulate action visually.
Monitoring user behavior across multiple devices for unified analytics used by advertisers.

D.definition: A daily spending limit for ad campaigns.
A digital catalogue containing detailed product data meant for affiliate marketing (e.g. images, descriptions, and pricing).
Scheduling ads to be strategically displayed during specific times of the day.
A tool to create deep links automatically.
A link directing users to a specific page deep into the advertiser's website content.
A platform allowing advertisers to programmatically buy ad space across multiple exchanges through a single interface.
A payment method billing users directly via mobile operator or internet service provider.
Direct purchase of ad space without the protection of an intermediary.
Transparency about ad or affiliate relationships.
Visual-based advertisements that appear on websites, apps, or social media platforms.
A search engine metric measuring the website's credibility and ranking potential.
A subscription confirmation process with two user interactions (e.g. email marketing).
Identical content found on multiple webpages negatively impacting the SEO potential.
Technology that creates personalized ad content in real-time based on user data.
User activity tracking that adjusts dynamically based on real-time actions.

E.definition: An effectiveness metric of how much users interact with an ad campaign, such as clicks and conversions.

F.definition: A tracking pixel that records a specific user action and offers insights into the effectiveness of an ad campaign.
Affiliate getting the commission by being the first click in a conversion path.
A fixed-rate payment for ad campaigns.
A feature limiting the number of times an ad is shown to the same user.

G.definition: Delivering ads to users based on their geographic location.

H.definition: A single request to load a webpage or resource.
A clickable link directing users to another page.

I.definition: A single instance of an ad (view) being displayed to a user.
Ad management and execution within an organization.
A non-intrusive ad format that mimics a push notification but appears within a webpage.
An incoming link from another website.
Traffic generated through rewards such as cash, discounts, or gifts.
Process of adding a website to a search engine database.
Full-screen ad format consisting of an image with a title and a description that appear a few moments after the website is opened.

K.definition: Metrics used to evaluate the success of an ad campaign or strategy, such as CTR or ROI.
Words or phrases users search for in search engines.

L.definition: A standalone webpage users land on after clicking an ad designed specifically to convert the visitors into leads and customers.
Affiliate getting the commission by being the last click in a conversion path.
A potential customer expressing interest in a product marketed via affiliates.
A revenue projection of the total value a customer can bring over their lifetime as a client.
A SEO strategy of building relevant hyperlinks to a website from external sources.
A group of users who share characteristics with the advertiser's existing partners and are more likely to be converted into customers.
Affiliates specializing in repeat customers and offering rewards in return for consistent actions.

M.definition: Maximum spending limit for ad campaigns.
A business selling products or services via affiliates.
A brief summary of a webpage's content that benefits the search engine results.

N.definition: Ads that blend into the surrounding content and mimick the style and tone of the source material, making them appear natural and non-disruptive.

O.definition: A SEO strategy performed outside of a website.
A specific promotion for products or services.
A SEO strategy optimizing the website content for higher ranking.
A link directing users away from a website.

P.definition: A small piece of code that tracks user behavior and conversions on websites.
Advertisers pay for each ad click.
A preliminary webpage before the main offer page.
A link to a specific product page.
The financial gain from revenues after expenses.
Automated buying and selling of ad inventory using algorithms.
An individual or company distributing content and embedding affiliate links within said content.
Ad unit sent directly to the user's devices or browsers after subscription that takes enough space to attract users' attention ideally.

R.definition: Unfiltered, raw data of clicks on a website.
A process of selling and buying ad inventory in real-time auctions.
A SEO strategy of websites exchanging links with one another.
Forwarding users from one URL to another.
An external domain that sends traffic to a target website.
A specific URL that sends traffic to a target website.
Ad inventory left unsold due to lower engagements and quality.
Serving ads to users who have previously visited a website or interacted with content.
A measure of how much profit an advertiser earns compared to their ad spend.
Income generated from goods or services.
An ad placement strategy where ads can appear anywhere within a network of websites rather than specific chosen places.

S.definition: Promoting websites through paid ads on search engine result pages.
Improving a website’s ranking in organic search results through strategic optimization.
A group of users categorized by shared characteristics for targeted advertising.
A platform that allows advertisers and publishers to manage their ad campaigns.

T.definition: Specific group of people an ad is designed to reach.
Data collected by an entity without direct interaction with the user, often aggregated from multiple sources.
A 1×1 image placed on a webpage to gather data on user behavior and conversions.
Origin from which users arrive at a website, such as other websites, search engines, or social media.

U.definition: Process of gaining new users for a platform, app, or service.
